The '''Porax-38 (P-38) starfighter''' was a tough, [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]-equipped [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] used by the [[Utapaun]]s as part of the [[Utapau Skyforce/Legends|Utapau Skyforce]]. Some models were adapted by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] into the [[Rogue-class Porax-38 starfighter/Legends|''Rogue''-class starfighter]] line.

==Characteristics==
[[File:P-38_2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Side view of a P-38]]
The Porax-38 had interstellar range and was fitted with highly effective, long-distance [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]] systems that provided early warning of enemy activity. Each sensor was mounted in a forward facing cone along with the scanning systems, and both were connected to powerful jamming devices. These large P-38 fighters were also capable of independent [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] jumps, which enabled them to defend [[Utapau/Legends|Utapau]] far into the [[Tarabba sector/Legends|Tarabba sector]]. Versatile thrusters, dual-[[Reactor/Legends|reactor]] systems, and high-capacity [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shielding]] made them a valuable asset to the Loyalist forces. Rather than using a single shield generator the ship had a deflector shield projector web. This web covered a large portion of the ship and had many individual [[Heat sink/Legends|heat sink]] cells embedded into it.

The interstellar range of these vessels also allowed them to take more punishment and they could endure longer, more arduous missions than tiny, fleet-based fighters like the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy's]] [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|''Vulture'' droid fighters]] and [[Droid tri-fighter/Legends|droid tri-fighters]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic's]] [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter/Legends|V-wings]] and [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|Eta-2s]].

The Porax-38 was manufactured by [[Buuper Torsckil Abbey Devices/Legends|Buuper Torsckil Abbey Devices]] and only required one [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], who had to wear a protective breathing mask. It had enough [[Foodstuff/Legends|consumables]] for 20 days of air, fuel, and food. Its primary armament was two High-power [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]]. Each laser cannon is connected to a beam-amplification chamber that severely increases the laser's intensity and heat damage output. The fighter's tiny cockpit could only seat one short, stocky [[Utai/Legends|Utai]], not the tall [[Pau'an/Legends|Pau'ans]]. Some Porax-38s had the cockpit modified for an [[IG-100 MagnaGuard/Legends|IG-100 MagnaGuard]].

==History==
[[File:Air_Commodore_Senin_Vant.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[Air Commodore/Legends|Air Commodore]] [[Senin Vant/Legends|Senin Vant]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] a P-38]]
The Utapaun navy was made up almost entirely of self made ships, and their largest [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|Rendili Dreadnought]] was barely one-fifth the size of a Trade Federation battleship. The Utapauns instead relied heavily on their P-38s as their primary means of planetary defense. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], several Porax-38 starfighters were captured by the Confederacy of Independent Systems and were subsequently modified to begin what became the [[Rogue-class Porax-38 starfighter/Legends|''Rogue''-class starfighter]] line.<ref name="TCW Incredible Vehicles">''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars: Incredible Vehicles]]''</ref>

The CIS [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Zolghast]] also owned a Porax-38, which he kept on [[Tirahnn/Legends|Tirahnn]] during the Separatist occupation of the world. However, it was captured by the Republic during the [[Battle of Tirahnn (Clone Wars)|Battle of Tirahnn]].<ref name="GAW">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref>

In the [[19 BBY/Legends|last year of the war]], [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|forces]] invaded Utapau. [[Tion Medon/Legends|Tion Medon]], the Master of Utapau Port Administration, ordered his outnumbered yet valuable P-38s into hiding, with the intention that the existence of the ships and their skilled pilots remain a secret until a better time to strike against the overwhelming might of the Separatists arose.

With the arrival of Republic forces under [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], Tion recalled his P-38s from their hidden hangars and set them to expelling the Separatist forces. The Utapau Skyforce [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] that engaged the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] [[Mankvim-814 light interceptor/Legends|light interceptors]] during Obi-Wan's arrival was led by the Utai [[Air Commodore/Legends|Air Commodore]] [[Senin Vant/Legends|Senin Vant]].

{{Ship-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
While ''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith Incredible Cross-Sections]]'' gives the maximum atmospheric speed of the P-38 as being 61,000 km/h, the later published [[Starship Battles (Star Wars Miniatures)|''Starship Battles'']] Preview 6 gives it as being 1,200 km/h. In addition to this, the former source gives the consumables rating as being 20 days whereas the latter gives it as being a mere 2 days.

The P-38 shares a number of characteristics with the real-world Lockheed P-38 Lightning, a World War II fighter aircraft. Apart from having the same designation/model number, the Porax-38 also possesses a similar layout; the engines being located in twin booms mounted one the wings, with the cockpit located in a centrally-mounted nacelle. Similar to the Lightning, the Porax-38 could also be classed as a "heavy" fighter, due to its toughness and firepower.

The starfighter was a late addition to the film and though a digital model was created, it was never completed to the level originally planned.<ref name="DB-P-38 starfighter">{{DB|starship|p38starfighter|P-38 starfighter}}</ref>

The episode guide for the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' episode [[Duel of the Droids]] refers to ''Rogue''-class starfighters as "P-38 fighters."<ref name="Duel of the Droids original Episode Guide">{{SWArchive|url=theclonewars/guide/episode007.html|text=''The Clone Wars'' Episode Guide: Duel of the Droids}}</ref>
